subject: Geography
title: Japan's Island Arc
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Japan's Island Arc.

hook.text: Japan is an archipelago of 6,852 islands formed by four colliding tectonic plates.

buildup.text: This makes it one of the most earthquake-prone nations on Earth—1,500 quakes a year.

discovery.text: Japan's isolation allowed a unique culture to develop during 250 years of self-imposed closure.

twist.text: The 2011 tsunami reached 40 meters high, devastating the Tōhoku coast.

climax.text: Japan engineered earthquake-resistant buildings that sway but rarely collapse.

punchline.text: Living on four colliding plates taught a nation to build for chaos.

quiz.question: How many islands make up Japan?
